channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: Development and Clinical Validation of an Automated Artificial Intelligence System for Bowel Wall Thickness Assessment in Adult Inflammatory Bowel Diseases.
item.description: Ultraschall in der Medizin (Stuttgart, Germany : 1980) - Intestinal ultrasound (IUS) is used to assess and monitor inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Bowel wall thickness (BWT) is its key marker, but accurate measurement is operator-dependent and requires...

item.title: Deep Learning-Driven Analysis and Quantification of Histopathologic Features in a Dextran Sulfate Sodium-Induced Colitis Mouse Model.
item.description: The American journal of pathology - A dextran-based colitis model has been used extensively to study the pathophysiology of inflammatory bowel disease, including ulcerative colitis and Crohn disease. Histologic studies of animal colo...

item.title: Conference Report: The FutuRE oF MinimalLy InvasivE GI and Capsule DiagnosTics (REFLECT), September 2025.
item.description: Diagnostics (Basel, Switzerland) - Capsule endoscopy (CE) is evolving from a primarily small-bowel imaging modality into a broader diagnostic platform that increasingly incorporates artificial intelligence (AI), robotic technologies...

item.title: Sub-inhibitory concentrations of metronidazole alter the proteomic profile and biological effects of extracellular vesicles from Clostridioides difficile.
item.description: BMC microbiology - Colonization with C. difficile is common in patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). The use of antibiotics can trigger the transition from colonization to active infection, complicating IBD...

item.title: Self-adaptive nanozyme-loaded yeast microcapsules for multifunctional and precise therapy of colitis via intestinal microenvironment remodeling.
item.description: Journal of nanobiotechnology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) management remains a significant challenge due to its complex pathogenesis. While current therapies for IBD are effective in symptom management, a radical cure rema...

item.title: Multidimensional dissection of shared genetic susceptibility in ulcerative colitis and colorectal cancer: novel insights from integrative single-cell and multi-omics analysis.
item.description: Inflammation research : official journal of the European Histamine Research Society ... [et al.] - Ulcerative colitis (UC) patients carry a 2.5-fold increased risk of colorectal cancer (CRC), yet the shared multi-scale genetic architecture remains poorly understood. We constructed an integrative...

item.title: The intestine as a nexus of tissue-encoded immunological memory.
item.description: International immunology - The intestine is continuously exposed to diverse microbial, dietary, and environmental signals, requiring tightly regulated immune responses that balance host defense and tolerance. Recent advances...

item.title: Expanding the therapeutic horizon in inflammatory bowel disease: The rise of non-cytokine compounds.
item.description: Biomedicine & pharmacotherapy = Biomedecine & pharmacotherapie - Review - While cytokine-targeted therapies have significantly transformed the treatment landscape of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), a substantial proportion of patients remain refractory or lose responsi...

item.title: Targeting the transcription factor GATA3 by the DNAzyme formulation SB012 in patients with moderately-to-severely active ulcerative colitis: a multicenter, randomized, placebo-controlled, phase 2a induction trial.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Augmented mucosal expression of the transcription factor GATA3 has been implicated in the pathogenesis of ulcerative colitis (UC). Here, we evaluated the efficacy and safety of SB012, an enema form...

item.title: Diagnosis and treatment of neonatal gastrointestinal diseases in extremely premature infants using ultrathin colonoscopy.
item.description: Pediatric research - BACKGROUND: This study aimed to evaluate the diagnostic and therapeutic utility of ultrathin colonoscopy for lower gastrointestinal disorders in neonates, including extremely preterm infants and to...

item.title: Comparison of Conventional versus Abbreviated MR Enterography: Assessing Disease Activity and Complications in Crohn Disease.
item.description: Radiology - Background MR enterography (MRE) is increasingly used to guide treatment and improve outcomes in Crohn disease (CD). An abbreviated MRE (A-MRE) protocol-omitting contrast and antiperistaltic agents...

item.title: Systemic Immune Signatures of Endoscopic-Histologic Discordance in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Pilot Study.
item.description: Journal of clinical medicine - Background: Endoscopic remission is a central therapeutic target in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD); however, histologic inflammation may persist despite apparent mucosal healing. The biological m...

item.title: Determinants of influenza vaccination uptake in older adults: findings from a retrospective cross-sectional study.
item.description: BMC public health - Influenza poses a serious health risk to older adults, and although annual vaccination is the most effective prevention, coverage in this population remains inconsistent. This study assessed influe...

item.title: Scrotal abscesses in perianal Crohn's disease: a retrospective cohort study of clinical presentation, management, and outcomes.
item.description: Techniques in coloproctology - Perianal Crohn's disease (pCD) is a severe phenotype frequently complicated by fistulas and recurrent abscesses. Although perianal sepsis is well described, genital involvement is rare, with scrota...

item.title: Incidence and prevalence of inflammatory Bowel disease in China: a regional population-based study from 2020 to 2022.
item.description: Scandinavian journal of gastroenterology - The global burden of inflammatory bowel diseases (IBDs), comprising Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), is on the rise. Studies regarding the incidence of IBD in China remain notably ...

item.title: Advanced therapies for extraintestinal manifestations of IBD: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Extraintestinal manifestations (EIMs) occur in one-fifth of patients with inflammatory bowel diseases (IBDs) (Crohn's disease[CD]; ulcerative colitis [UC]). As advanced therapies (ATs) for IBD beco...

item.title: Colorectal dysplasia and malignancy outcomes in primary sclerosing cholangitis and inflammatory bowel disease requiring liver transplantation and colectomy: A multicenter study.
item.description: Surgery - Literature guiding management of patients with primary sclerosing cholangitis and inflammatory bowel disease undergoing liver transplantation for primary sclerosing cholangitis and total abdominal ...

item.title: Orbital myositis last year and now Crohn's disease: A case report of a rare association.
item.description: SAGE open medical case reports - Case report - Crohn's disease is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease in which extraintestinal manifestations may occur before the onset of intestinal symptoms. Ocular manifestations, such as orbital myositis, a...

item.title: The IL-10/IL-10Rα axis in fibroblasts limits large intestinal pathology by suppressing type I interferon signaling.
item.description: International immunology - Recent studies identified that the dysregulation of fibroblast activity, in addition to impairment in epithelial integrity and uncontrolled immune response, is implicated in the pathogenesis of inf...

item.title: The multifaceted regulation of autophagy protein ATG16L1 and its implications in human diseases.
item.description: Autophagy - Review - ATG16L1 (autophagy related 16 like 1) is a core macroautophagy/autophagy protein essential for autophagosome formation. It also functions in non-canonical autophagy pathways such as LC3-associated ...
